summary: Matched the Mr. Game & Watch turtle item source by replacing the hand-written assembly object with C, moving the build configuration from asm to src, and relocating turtle-specific prototypes from the broad it_27CF.h header into itgamewatchturtle.h. The implementation reconstructs spawning, state table callbacks, owner/turtle flag interactions, animation transitions, and simple wrapper callbacks around existing item helpers.
what_changed: Deleted the 241-line assembly file asm/melee/it/items/itgamewatchturtle.s after matching the item in C.
Added src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchturtle.c with C implementations for it_802C6F40, it_802C7044, it_802C7074, it_802C70C0, it_802C70E0, it_802C7100, it_802C7158, it_802C7180, and it_802C71EC.
Defined ItemStateTable it_803F7918 in C with two entries, both using it_802C7180 as the callback corresponding to the deleted assembly data table.
Changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itgamewatchturtle.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itgamewatchturtle.c").
Changed obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchturtle.c.o instead of the old asm object.
Moved turtle item function declarations out of the aggregate item header src/melee/it/items/it_27CF.h and into src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchturtle.h.
Added an include of it/items/itgamewatchturtle.h to ftGw_AttackAir.c so Game & Watch attack-air code references the item-specific header directly.
smart_moves: Kept the item-specific API in itgamewatchturtle.h rather than leaving matched turtle declarations in the broad it_27CF.h bucket, improving ownership and reducing header clutter.
Preserved the original assembly behavior by mapping obvious one-call functions directly to existing helpers: it_802C70C0 to it_8026B724, it_802C70E0 to it_8026B73C, and it_802C71EC to it_8026B894.

Reconstructed the spawn function around SpawnItem fields instead of manual stack layout, including kind It_Kind_GameWatch_Turtle, parent gobj fields, copied position, facing direction, zero velocity, zero damage, and x44_flag.bits.b0.
Matched owner-dependent Game & Watch callbacks by checking it->owner before calling ftGw_AttackAirN_ItemTurtleSetFlag or ftGw_AttackAirN_ItemCheckTurtleRemove.
Retained matching-sensitive unused locals in C, such as u32 unused in it_802C7074 and u32 unused[2] in it_802C7180, indicating stack-shape preservation was needed.
decomp_lessons: When replacing an asm item file with C, update both the object list and configure.py status; deleting the asm file alone is not sufficient.
Matched item modules should expose their own prototypes from the item-specific header and remove those prototypes from catch-all headers when possible.
Item callbacks often mirror tiny wrappers around shared item helpers; identifying these wrappers can quickly retire assembly without overcomplicating the C.
For Game & Watch generated items, the item owner pointer at the Item level is used to communicate back to fighter code; null-owner paths must be preserved exactly.
Stack-shaping dummy locals may be necessary even in small item callbacks to match generated code.
naming_conventions: The PR retained address-based symbol names such as it_802C6F40 and it_802C7180 because no semantic names were introduced in the evidence.
Function parameters in the new C use descriptive names where roles are known, such as parent, pos, dir, item_gobj, and spawn_item.
The item state table symbol remains address-based as it_803F7918.
Header declarations use typed aliases from item/fighter code, including Item_GObj, HSD_GObj, Vec3, u8, f32, and bool.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated the deleted assembly .data table into a C ItemStateTable array with matching callback slots.
Used SpawnItem field assignments to reproduce the stack-built structure passed to Item_80268B18.
Used GET_ITEM(item_gobj) for repeated lwz 0x2c item-data accesses from the assembly.
Preserved ternary-style control flow in it_802C7180: if owner exists, call ftGw_AttackAirN_ItemCheckTurtleRemove(owner); otherwise treat the item as removable.

Used existing item constants and helpers, including It_Kind_GameWatch_Turtle, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, Item_80268B18, Item_8026AB54, Item_80268E5C, Item_802694CC, Item_8026A8EC, it_8026B724, it_8026B73C, and it_8026B894.
Kept an explicit extern declaration for it_8027CE64 in the C file, suggesting the available headers did not yet provide a suitable declaration.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename the address-based itgamewatchturtle functions to semantic names.
Check whether it_8027CE64 eventually receives a proper header declaration instead of a local extern.
Compare other Mr. Game & Watch item matches, such as itgamewatchfire, itgamewatchparachute, itgamewatchbreath, and itgamewatchjudge, for reusable SpawnItem and owner-callback patterns.
Search for other PRs moving item declarations out of it_27CF.h into item-specific headers.
